content:
Actually, John Maynard Keynes never won a Nobel Prize in Economics. The Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences was established in 1968, long after Keynes' death in 1946. Keynes is one of the most influential economists in history, known for his work on macroeconomics and government intervention, but he did not receive the Nobel Prize.

If you want, I can provide you with examples of American economists who have won the Nobel Prize in Economics. Would you like that?
